name: Peloton Interactive, Inc.

profile.description: Peloton Interactive, Inc. provides fitness and wellness products and services in North America and internationally. The company offers connected fitness products, such as the Peloton Bike, Peloton Bike+, Peloton Tread, Peloton Tread+, and Peloton Row. It also provides all-access, rental, and Peloton App memberships. In addition, the company leases Peloton Bike+ products. The company sells its products through e-commerce and inside sales, retail showrooms, and third-party retailers. Peloton Interactive, Inc. was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Peloton created the connected fitness category but has struggled to innovate beyond the original product. Revenue declining 2% FY2026 [1]. AI Deployment (1/2): AI-powered personalization for workout recommendations and adaptive training. Market Share (1/2): Category leader in connected fitness with 2.66M paid subscribers, though declining. Pipeline (0/2): No transformative products. R&D Intensity (1/1): Continued investment in content and software.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Peloton's founding mission to make fitness accessible and inspiring is genuinely reflected in the product experience. Strong instructor-led community creates emotional connection [1]. Community Impact (1/2): Fitness accessibility through app-based subscription. Employee Retention (0/2): Multiple rounds of layoffs. Insider Buying (1/2): Some management equity stakes.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q3 FY2026 revenue of $630.9M was up 1% YoY — first growth in some time [1]. Subscription revenue grew 4% QoQ. GAAP profitable for third time in four quarters. Full-year guidance of $2.42-2.44B implies -2% annual decline. Average monthly churn improved to 1.2%. Turning the corner on profitability but subscribers still declining (2.66M).

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Connected fitness hardware is physical and creates switching costs. However, AI-powered virtual fitness coaches and personalized workout apps could reduce the value of Peloton's instructor-led model [1]. The hardware + subscription bundle provides some defensibility. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI personalization in workout recommendations generating user engagement.

infinite_game.summary: Peloton was founded with genuine infinite game purpose but pandemic-era overexpansion and multiple CEO changes suggest finite game execution. The current profit-focused reset prioritizes survival over purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Making fitness accessible and inspiring is genuine and reflected in the product [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Community and instructor culture is strong despite corporate layoffs.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Acknowledges Apple Fitness+ and Lululemon/Mirror.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Multiple CEO changes and reactive cost-cutting suggest lack of courageous leadership.

investment_thesis: Peloton achieved Q3 FY2026 revenue of $630.9M with first YoY growth and GAAP profitability for the third time in four quarters [1]. Subscription revenue grew 4% QoQ and churn improved to 1.2%. The profit-focused reset under current management is stabilizing the business. At $6.40, significant recovery potential if subscriber trends stabilize.
thesis_continued: Paid subscribers declined to 2.66M, and full-year guidance of $2.42-2.44B implies a 2% revenue decline [1]. The connected fitness market has matured with Apple, Lululemon, and free alternatives competing for attention. Peloton's premium pricing faces consumer spending pressure. Multiple CEO transitions have eroded strategic continuity. The company has yet to demonstrate it can grow subscribers again.
key_catalysts: Subscriber decline stabilization and eventual growth resumption as churn improves from 1.2%
Subscription revenue growth of 4% QoQ accelerating as new content and features attract users
Sustained GAAP profitability validating the restructured business model
key_risks: Subscriber declines continuing below 2.66M as connected fitness market matures
Apple Fitness+ and free workout alternatives eroding Peloton's premium positioning
Full-year revenue declining 2% suggesting the business hasn't fully stabilized
sources: Peloton Q3 FY2026 Earnings Release, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — PTON Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
